commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=63b5c6d3b87fabd7dab2a9ad5b5e40d11a57006b)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a built-in W&B/CoreWeave Sandboxes delegated-run provider with native Go gRPC client, vendored v1beta2 protos, config/env/flags, docs, unit tests, and live smoke coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. The changed behavior is source-verifiable from the diff, and the PR body/comments provide live output for the new provider path.
commands.review_followup: A human maintainer should decide whether this beta W&B/CoreWeave provider belongs in core; the remaining docs issue is mechanical but not the only merge consideration.

target.title: feat(wandb): native gRPC sandbox provider (W&B by CoreWeave)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the **W&B Sandboxes** provider (`provider: wandb`, alias `weights-and-biases`) — [Weights & Biases](https://wandb.ai/) sandboxes, now operated by CoreWeave post-2025 acquisition. Native Go gRPC client against `coreweave.sandbox.v1beta2`; no Python in the binary.

The reason this provider matters: it accepts the `WANDB_API_KEY` an ML engineer already has in `~/.netrc` from `wandb login`. For users already on W&B, that is zero new accounts, zero new credentials, zero billing setup.

## Commit map

1. `proto(wandb): vendor coreweave.sandbox.v1beta2 + generate Go stubs` — protos from `buf.build/coreweave/sandbox`, googleapis pinned in `buf.lock`.
2. `feat(wandb): native gRPC sandbox provider (W&B by CoreWeave)` — the implementation.
3. `test(wandb): unit coverage + opt-in live smoke against the gateway` — `fakeWandbAPI` seam + `-tags smoke` live target.
4. `docs(wandb): provider README + index entries (W&B by CoreWeave)` — provider doc + index updates.
5. `refactor(wandb): align Doctor with the inventory-style native providers` — drop shim-era cruft (-65 lines).

## Verified end-to-end

```
=== RUN   TestSmokeVersionAndExec
    client_smoke_test.go:42: connected; api version = coreweave.sandbox.v1beta2
=== RUN   TestSmokeVersionAndExec/AcquireExecStop
    client_smoke_test.go:58: acquired sandbox id=3ad3da87-... status=SANDBOX_STATUS_RUNNING
    client_smoke_test.go:81: exec exit=0 stdout="hello-from-crabbox\nuid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)\n"
    client_smoke_test.go:66: stopped sandbox 3ad3da87-...
--- PASS: TestSmokeVersionAndExec (5.84s)
```

```
export CRABBOX_WANDB_API_KEY=...
go test -tags smoke -run TestSmokeVersionAndExec -v ./internal/providers/wandb/
```

## Design notes

- **Transport:** gRPC over HTTP/2 + TLS. Default endpoint `api.cwsandbox.com:443`, overridable via `CWSANDBOX_BASE_URL`.
- **Auth:** three gRPC metadata headers — `x-wandb-api-key`, `x-entity-id`, `x-project-name` — via `grpc.WithUnaryInterceptor`.
- **Credential precedence:** `CRABBOX_WANDB_API_KEY` → `cfg.Wandb.APIKey` → `WANDB_API_KEY` → `~/.netrc`.
- **Connection lifecycle:** one `*grpc.ClientConn` per backend instance (HTTP/2 multiplexes).
- **Billing safety:** default `max_lifetime_seconds = 1800` (30 min). `--wandb-max-lifetime`.
- **Error mapping:** gRPC `codes.*` → typed `*wandbAPIError` with sysexits.h-aligned exit codes.
- **Message-size cap:** 128 MiB on send/recv (default 4 MiB trips `RetrieveFile`).
- **Keepalive:** 30s/10s with `PermitWithoutStream` for NAT/LB resilience.

## Test plan

- `go build ./...` clean
- `go vet ./...` clean
- `go test -short ./...` green
- Live smoke `Acquire → Exec → Stop` against `api.cwsandbox.com:443`
- Credential precedence exercised
- Doctor matches modal/e2b/runpod inventory shape
target.author: zozo123
